ecu_manufacturers.name: Robert Bosch GmbH
ecu_manufacturers.description: One of the world's leading automotive suppliers, offering a wide range of ECUs for engine management, driver assistance, and infotainment systems. A dominant force in the global ECU market [citation:1][citation:2][citation:6].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Continental AG
ecu_manufacturers.description: A major German automotive technology company providing ECUs for powertrain, chassis and safety, and user experience applications [citation:1][citation:2][citation:6].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Denso Corporation
ecu_manufacturers.description: A leading global automotive parts supplier headquartered in Kariya, Japan. It designs and manufactures ECUs for engine control, driver assistance, HVAC, and hybrid system control [citation:1][citation:2][citation:9].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Delphi Technologies (BorgWarner)
ecu_manufacturers.description: Now a part of BorgWarner, Delphi Technologies specializes in advanced powertrain ECUs, emissions management, and vehicle connectivity solutions [citation:2][citation:5][citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: ZF Friedrichshafen AG
ecu_manufacturers.description: A global technology company that supplies ECUs for transmission, steering, safety systems, and other critical automotive functions [citation:1][citation:2][citation:6].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Mitsubishi Electric Corporation
ecu_manufacturers.description: A major Japanese electronics and electrical equipment manufacturer that produces ECUs for various automotive applications [citation:1][citation:5][citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Hitachi Astemo, Ltd.
ecu_manufacturers.description: A mobility technology company formed from the consolidation of Hitachi Automotive Systems and other entities. It supplies ECUs for powertrain, chassis, and ADAS functions [citation:1][citation:2][citation:5].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Magneti Marelli (Calsonic Kansei)
ecu_manufacturers.description: An Italian automotive components and systems supplier. It offers ECUs for powertrain, lighting, and infotainment technologies [citation:1][citation:2][citation:5].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Hyundai Mobis
ecu_manufacturers.description: The largest automotive parts supplier in South Korea, providing ECUs and other electronic components to Hyundai, Kia, and other global OEMs [citation:1][citation:6][citation:9].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Vitesco Technologies
ecu_manufacturers.description: Spun off from Continental AG, Vitesco Technologies specializes in electrification and powertrain solutions, including advanced ECUs for engine management, hybrid systems, and battery control [citation:1][citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Aptiv PLC
ecu_manufacturers.description: Formerly Delphi Automotive, Aptiv is a global technology company focused on advanced safety, user experience, and electrical architecture. Supplies ECUs for ADAS, connectivity, and power distribution systems [citation:1][citation:3][citation:11].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Aisin Corporation
ecu_manufacturers.description: A Toyota Group supplier producing ECUs for powertrain, transmission, hybrid systems, and advanced driver assistance. A major Tier 1 supplier with global manufacturing footprint [citation:2][citation:26].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Magna International Inc.
ecu_manufacturers.description: One of the world's largest automotive suppliers with capabilities in ADAS, electrification, and vehicle systems. Produces ECUs for body control, lighting, and advanced safety applications [citation:22][citation:25].

ecu_manufacturers.name: UAES (United Automotive Electronic Systems)
ecu_manufacturers.description: A leading Chinese joint venture between ZF and SAIC Motor, specializing in powertrain and engine management systems for the local market [citation:5][citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Weifu Group
ecu_manufacturers.description: A major Chinese automotive components manufacturer, known for its diesel engine fuel injection systems and ECUs [citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Valeo
ecu_manufacturers.description: A global automotive supplier focused on intuitive driving and CO2 reduction, with ECUs playing a key role in its thermal, powertrain, and comfort & driving assistance systems [citation:5].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Autoliv
ecu_manufacturers.description: A global leader in automotive safety systems, including electronic control units for airbags, seatbelts, and other safety restraint systems [citation:1][citation:6].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Lear Corporation
ecu_manufacturers.description: A major American automotive supplier that produces ECUs primarily for seating and electrical distribution systems, including body control modules [citation:1][citation:9].

ecu_manufacturers.name: LinControl
ecu_manufacturers.description: A Chinese company specializing in automotive electronic control systems, including ECUs for engine and transmission management [citation:5][citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Hyundai Kefico / Hyundai Autron
ecu_manufacturers.description: A key Hyundai Motor Group supplier specializing in powertrain ECUs, engine management, and emissions control systems for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis vehicles [citation:1][citation:3].

ecu_manufacturers.name: NEDEC (Nissan Electronics)
ecu_manufacturers.description: Nissan-affiliated electronics supplier producing ECUs for engine control, body electronics, and advanced driver assistance systems primarily for Nissan and Renault vehicles [citation:1][citation:7].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Sensata Technologies
ecu_manufacturers.description: A mid-size supplier of automotive sensors and control modules, producing ECUs for tire-pressure monitoring, battery management, and high-voltage junction systems with global OEM partnerships [citation:extracted].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Rockwell Collins (Collins Aerospace)
ecu_manufacturers.description: A major aerospace and defense company that develops engine control units for aviation applications, distinguishing itself from purely automotive manufacturers [citation:1][citation:5].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Changzhou Yikong (Econtrols)
ecu_manufacturers.description: A high-tech Chinese enterprise focused on automotive electronic control technology. Specializes in engine and power system control for diesel, hybrid, and electric vehicles [citation:3][citation:5].

ecu_manufacturers.name: T-Engineering
ecu_manufacturers.description: An engineering company providing in-house engine control systems, vehicle control units, and body control modules, with deep roots in the SAAB Trionic-era [citation:4].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Ecotron
ecu_manufacturers.description: A California-based manufacturer specializing in electronic fuel injection (EFI) systems and engine control units for small and special engines, including those used in UAVs and watercraft [citation:8].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Life Racing
ecu_manufacturers.description: A niche manufacturer known for producing high-performance, motorsport-focused engine control units and data acquisition systems [citation:5].

ecu_manufacturers.name: TTTech Auto
ecu_manufacturers.description: Specializes in safety-critical automotive platforms and domain controller ECUs for ADAS and autonomous driving. Partnered with Audi, Aptiv, and NXP for ASIL-D compliant systems [citation:extracted].

ecu_manufacturers.name: Transtron Inc.
ecu_manufacturers.description: A joint venture of Fujitsu and Isuzu specializing in automotive control units for commercial vehicles, including engine ECUs for diesel applications and in-vehicle information systems [citation:extracted].

ecu_manufacturers.name: New Eagle
ecu_manufacturers.description: A niche ECU supplier providing production-ready automotive ECUs and software tools to small OEMs and startups, with ISO 26262-capable units and Tier-1 partnerships [citation:extracted].
